records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3593 - It is narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from his father, who said:
"I saw Abu Al-Hasan (as) working in his land, and his feet were soaked in sweat. I said to him, 'May I be your ransom! Where are the men (to assist you)?'
Imam (as) replied: 'O Ali, one who is better than me and my father worked with his hands in his land.'
I asked him, 'Who is that?'
He said: 'The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), Commander of the Faithful, and my forefathers (peace be upon them)-all of them worked with their hands, and it is the work of the prophets, the messengers, and the righteous."
records.arabicText: 3593 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَعْمَلُ فِي أَرْضٍ لَهُ وَ قَدِ اِسْتَنْقَعَتْ قَدَمَاهُ فِي اَلْعَرَقِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَيْنَ اَلرِّجَالُ فَقَالَ "يَا عَلِيُّ عَمِلَ بِالْيَدِ مَنْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِنِّي وَ مِنْ أَبِي فِي أَرْضِهِ" فَقُلْتُ لَهُ مَنْ هُوَ فَقَالَ " رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ آبَائِي عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ كُلُّهُمْ قَدْ عَمِلُوا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَ هُوَ مِنْ عَمَلِ اَلنَّبِيِّينَ وَ اَلْمُرْسَلِينَ وَ اَلصَّالِحِينَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3593 - It is narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from his father, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I saw Abu Al-Hasan (as) working in his land, and his feet were soaked in sweat. I said to him, 'May I be your ransom! Where are the men (to assist you)?'
Imam (as) replied: 'O Ali, one who is better than me and my father worked with his hands in his land.'
I asked him, 'Who is that?'
He said: 'The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), Commander of the Faithful, and my forefathers (peace be upon them)-all of them worked with their hands, and it is the work of the prophets, the messengers, and the righteous."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3594 - It is narrated from Sharif ibn Sabiq Al-Taflisi, from Al-Fadl ibn Abi Qurrah Al-Samandi Al-Kufi, from Abu Abdullah (as), that Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
"Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, revealed to Dawud (as): 'You are an excellent servant, except that you eat from the public treasury and do not work with your hands.'
Dawud (as) then wept.
So Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, revealed to the iron:
'Soften for My servant Dawud.' (Surah Saba 34:10)
And the iron softened.
Allah (swt) made the iron pliable for him, and he began to make a coat of armor each day, selling it for a thousand dirhams.
Dawud (as) made three hundred and sixty coats of armor, selling them for three hundred and sixty thousand dirhams, and he became independent of the public treasury."
records.arabicText:
3594 - وَ رَوَى شَرِيفُ بْنُ سَابِقٍ اَلتَّفْلِيسِيُّ عَنِ اَلْفَضْلِ بْنِ أَبِي قُرَّةَ اَلسَّمَنْدِيِّ اَلْكُوفِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
"أَوْحَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى دَاوُدَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ " "أَنَّكَ نِعْمَ اَلْعَبْدُ لَوْ لاَ أَنَّكَ تَأْكُلُ مِنْ بَيْتِ اَلْمَالِ وَ لاَ تَعْمَلُ بِيَدِكَ شَيْئاً" قَالَ "فَبَكَى دَاوُدُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَوْحَى اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى اَلْحَدِيدِ ۝ أَنْ لِنْ لِعَبْدِي دَاوُدَ ۝
فَلاَنَ فَأَلاَنَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى لَهُ اَلْحَدِيدَ فَكَانَ يَعْمَلُ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ دِرْعاً فَيَبِيعُهَا بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ فَعَمِلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ثَلاَثَمِائَةٍ وَ سِتِّينَ دِرْعاً فَبَاعَهَا بِثَلاَثِمِائَةٍ وَ سِتِّينَ أَلْفاً وَ اِسْتَغْنَى عَنْ بَيْتِ اَلْمَالِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3595 - It is narrated from Al-Fadl ibn Abi Qurrah:
"We entered upon Abu Abdullah (as) while he was working in his garden.
We said: 'May Allah (swt) make us your ransom! Let us work for you, or let the servants handle it.'
Imam (as) replied: 'No, leave me to it, for I desire that Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, sees me working with my own hands and seeking lawful sustenance, even if it causes hardship to myself.'"
records.arabicText: 3595 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْفَضْلِ بْنِ أَبِي قُرَّةَ قَالَ : دَخَلْنَا عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ هُوَ يَعْمَلُ فِي حَائِطٍ لَهُ فَقُلْنَا جَعَلَنَا اَللَّهُ فِدَاكَ دَعْنَا نَعْمَلْ لَكَ أَوْ تَعْمَلْهُ اَلْغِلْمَانُ قَالَ "لاَ دَعُونِي فَإِنِّي أَشْتَهِي أَنْ يَرَانِيَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَعْمَلُ بِيَدِي وَ أَطْلُبُ اَلْحَلاَلَ فِي أَذَى نَفْسِي".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3595 - It is narrated from Al-Fadl ibn Abi Qurrah:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"We entered upon Abu Abdullah (as) while he was working in his garden.
We said: 'May Allah (swt) make us your ransom! Let us work for you, or let the servants handle it.'
Imam (as) replied: 'No, leave me to it, for I desire that Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, sees me working with my own hands and seeking lawful sustenance, even if it causes hardship to myself.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3596 - It is reported that Commander of the Faithful (as) used to go out during the midday heat for tasks that had already been taken care of, desiring that Allah (swt), the Exalted, would see him exerting himself in the pursuit of lawful sustenance.
records.arabicText: 3596 - وَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَخْرُجُ فِي اَلْهَاجِرَةِ فِي اَلْحَاجَةِ قَدْ كُفِيَهَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَرَاهُ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى يُتْعِبُ نَفْسَهُ فِي طَلَبِ اَلْحَلاَلِ.

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3597 - It is reported from al-Fadl ibn Abi Qurrah that he said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as), "These people claim that the earnings of a teacher are unlawful."
Imam (as) replied: "The enemies of Allah (swt) have lied! They only intend to prevent their children from learning the Quran. If a man were to give the teacher the blood money of his child, it would be lawful for the teacher."
records.arabicText:
3597 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْفَضْلِ بْنِ أَبِي قُرَّةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ هَؤُلاَءِ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ كَسْبَ اَلْمُعَلِّمِ سُحْتٌ فَقَالَ
"كَذَبَ أَعْدَاءُ اَللَّهِ إِنَّمَا أَرَادُوا أَنْ لاَ يُعَلِّمُوا أَوْلاَدَهُمُ اَلْقُرْآنَ لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً أَعْطَى اَلْمُعَلِّمَ دِيَةَ وَلَدِهِ كَانَ لِلْمُعَلِّمِ مُبَاحاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3597 - It is reported from al-Fadl ibn Abi Qurrah that he said:
I asked Abu Abdillah (as), "These people claim that the earnings of a teacher are unlawful."
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The enemies of Allah (swt) have lied! They only intend to prevent their children from learning the Quran. If a man were to give the teacher the blood money of his child, it would be lawful for the teacher."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3598 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
"Part of a man's happiness is that his business is in his own land, that his associates are righteous, and that he has children who assist him."
records.arabicText: 3598 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ مِنْ سَعَادَةِ اَلْمَرْءِ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَتْجَرُهُ فِي بِلاَدِهِ وَ يَكُونَ خُلَطَاؤُهُ صَالِحِينَ وَ يَكُونَ لَهُ أَوْلاَدٌ يَسْتَعِينُ بِهِمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3598 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Part of a man's happiness is that his business is in his own land, that his associates are righteous, and that he has children who assist him."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3599 - It is narrated from Abdul-Hamid ibn 'Awad al-Ta'i who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have set up a millstone, and it is my sitting place where my companions also sit with me."
Imam (as) said: "That is Allah's (swt) mercy."
records.arabicText:
3599 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ بْنِ عَوَّاضٍ اَلطَّائِيِّ قَالَ :
قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي اِتَّخَذْتُ رَحًى فِيهَا مَجْلِسِي وَ يَجْلِسُ إِلَيَّ فِيهَا أَصْحَابِي قَالَ "ذَاكَ رِفْقُ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3599 - It is narrated from Abdul-Hamid ibn 'Awad al-Ta'i who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have set up a millstone, and it is my sitting place where my companions also sit with me."
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "That is Allah's (swt) mercy."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3600 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said to Al-Walid ibn Subayh:
"O Walid, do not buy anything for me from one who is engaged in unlawful earnings (Muharif), for there is no blessing in associating with him."
records.arabicText: 3600 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِلْوَلِيدِ بْنِ صَبِيحٍ : "يَا وَلِيدُ لاَ تَشْتَرِ لِي مِنْ مُحَارَفٍ شَيْئاً فَإِنَّ خُلْطَتَهُ لاَ بَرَكَةَ فِيهَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3600 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said to Al-Walid ibn Subayh:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O Walid, do not buy anything for me from one who is engaged in unlawful earnings (Muharif), for there is no blessing in associating with him."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3601 - Imam (as) said: "Do not associate with or conduct business except with those who have been raised in goodness."
records.arabicText: 3601 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ تُخَالِطُوا وَ لاَ تُعَامِلُوا إِلاَّ مَنْ نَشَأَ فِي اَلْخَيْرِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3601 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not associate with or conduct business except with those who have been raised in goodness."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3602 - Imam (as) said: "Beware of dealing with those who have defects, for they are the most unjust of people."
records.arabicText: 3602 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اِحْذَرُوا مُعَامَلَةَ أَصْحَابِ اَلْعَاهَاتِ فَإِنَّهُمْ أَظْلَمُ شَيْءٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3602 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Beware of dealing with those who have defects, for they are the most unjust of people."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3603 - Imam (as) said to Abu Al-Rabi' Al-Shami:
"Do not associate with the Kurds, for the Kurds are a group from the Jinn whom Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, has removed the veil from."
records.arabicText: 3603 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِأَبِي اَلرَّبِيعِ اَلشَّامِيِّ : "لاَ تُخَالِطِ اَلْأَكْرَادَ فَإِنَّ اَلْأَكْرَادَ حَيٌّ مِنَ اَلْجِنِّ كَشَفَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَنْهُمُ اَلْغِطَاءَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3603 - Imam (as) said to Abu Al-Rabi' Al-Shami:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not associate with the Kurds, for the Kurds are a group from the Jinn whom Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, has removed the veil from."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3604 - Imam (as) said:
"Do not seek the assistance of a Magian, even for holding the legs of your sheep while you intend to slaughter it."
records.arabicText: 3604 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ تَسْتَعِنْ بِمَجُوسِيٍّ ، وَ لَوْ عَلَى أَخْذِ قَوَائِمِ شَاتِكَ وَ أَنْتَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَذْبَحَهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3604 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not seek the assistance of a Magian, even for holding the legs of your sheep while you intend to slaughter it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3605 - Imam (as) said: "Beware of associating with the lowly, for it does not lead to any good."
records.arabicText: 3605 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِيَّاكُمْ وَ مُخَالَطَةَ اَلسَّفِلَةِ، فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَئُولُ إِلَى خَيْرٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3605 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Beware of associating with the lowly, for it does not lead to any good."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3606 - Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasaar asked Abu Abdullah (as): "I have abandoned trade."
Imam (as) replied: "Do not do that. Open your door, spread your mat, and seek sustenance from your Lord (azj), Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 3606 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ بْنِ يَسَارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنِّي قَدْ تَرَكْتُ اَلتِّجَارَةَ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَفْعَلْ اِفْتَحْ بَابَكَ وَ اُبْسُطْ بِسَاطَكَ وَ اِسْتَرْزِقِ اَللَّهَ رَبَّكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3606 - Al-Fudhayl ibn Yasaar asked Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I have abandoned trade."
Imam (as) replied: "Do not do that. Open your door, spread your mat, and seek sustenance from your Lord (azj), Allah (swt)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3607 - Sadir Al-Sayrafi said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as): "What is required of a man in seeking sustenance?"
Imam (as) replied: "O Sadir, if you open your door and spread your mat, then you have fulfilled what is required of you."
records.arabicText: 3607 - وَ قَالَ سَدِيرٌ اَلصَّيْرَفِيُّ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ عَلَى اَلرَّجُلِ فِي طَلَبِ اَلرِّزْقِ فَقَالَ "يَا سَدِيرُ إِذَا فَتَحْتَ بَابَكَ وَ بَسَطْتَ بِسَاطَكَ فَقَدْ قَضَيْتَ مَا عَلَيْكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3607 - Sadir Al-Sayrafi said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as): "What is required of a man in seeking sustenance?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O Sadir, if you open your door and spread your mat, then you have fulfilled what is required of you."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3608 - Imam (as) said: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has made the sustenance of the believers come from where they do not expect. This is because when a servant does not know the source of his sustenance, his supplications increase."
records.arabicText: 3608 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى جَعَلَ أَرْزَاقَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ يَحْتَسِبُونَ وَ ذَلِكَ أَنَّ اَلْعَبْدَ إِذَا لَمْ يَعْرِفْ وَجْهَ رِزْقِهِ كَثُرَ دُعَاؤُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3608 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has made the sustenance of the believers come from where they do not expect. This is because when a servant does not know the source of his sustenance, his supplications increase."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3609 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "Be more hopeful for what you do not expect than for what you expect. Indeed, Moses, son of Imran (as), went out to fetch fire for his family, but Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, spoke to him, and he returned as a prophet. And the Queen of Sheba went out and embraced Islam with Solomon (as). And the magicians of Pharaoh went out seeking honor for Pharaoh but returned as believers."
records.arabicText: 3609 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُنْ لِمَا لاَ تَرْجُو أَرْجَى مِنْكَ لِمَا تَرْجُو فَإِنَّ مُوسَى بْنَ عِمْرَانَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ خَرَجَ يَقْتَبِسُ لِأَهْلِهِ نَاراً فَكَلَّمَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ رَجَعَ نَبِيّاً وَ خَرَجَتْ مَلِكَةُ، سَبَإٍ فَأَسْلَمَتْ مَعَ سُلَيْمَانَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ خَرَجَتْ سَحَرَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ يَطْلُبُونَ اَلْعِزَّةَ لِفِرْعَوْنَ فَرَجَعُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3609 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Be more hopeful for what you do not expect than for what you expect. Indeed, Moses, son of Imran (as), went out to fetch fire for his family, but Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, spoke to him, and he returned as a prophet. And the Queen of Sheba went out and embraced Islam with Solomon (as). And the magicians of Pharaoh went out seeking honor for Pharaoh but returned as believers."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3610 - A man said to Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
"Promise me something."
Imam (as) replied: "How can I promise you, when I have more hope in what I do not expect than in what I do expect?"
records.arabicText: 3610 - وَ قَالَ رَجُلٌ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ عِدْنِي قَالَ "كَيْفَ أَعِدُكَ وَ أَنَا لِمَا لاَ أَرْجُو أَرْجَى مِنِّي لِمَا أَرْجُو".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3610 - A man said to Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Promise me something."
Imam (as) replied: "How can I promise you, when I have more hope in what I do not expect than in what I do expect?"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3611 - It is narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Abdullah (as):
"Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, does not close a door of sustenance for a believer except that He opens for him what is better than it."
records.arabicText: 3611 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَا سَدَّ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَى مُؤْمِنٍ بَابَ رِزْقٍ إِلاَّ فَتَحَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ مَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3611 - It is narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, does not close a door of sustenance for a believer except that He opens for him what is better than it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3612 - It is narrated by Al-Sakooni, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them) that
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Whoever receives sustenance from Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, without having walked towards it with his feet, nor extended his hand for it, nor spoken about it with his tongue, nor prepared himself for it by tightening his garments, nor exposed himself to acquire it-he is among those whom Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, mentioned in His Book:
'And whoever fears Allah (swt), He will make a way out for him, and will provide for him from where he does not expect.'" (Surah At-Talaq 65:2-3)
records.arabicText: 3612 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ أَتَاهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِرِزْقٍ لَمْ يَخْطُ إِلَيْهِ بِرِجْلِهِ وَ لَمْ يَمُدَّ إِلَيْهِ يَدَهُ وَ لَمْ يَتَكَلَّمْ فِيهِ بِلِسَانِهِ وَ لَمْ يَشُدَّ إِلَيْهِ ثِيَابَهُ وَ لَمْ يَتَعَرَّضْ لَهُ كَانَ مِمَّنْ ذَكَرَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي كِتَابِهِ: ۝ وَ مَنْ يَتَّقِ اَللّٰهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً وَ يَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لاٰ يَحْتَسِبُ ۝ "
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3612 - It is narrated by Al-Sakooni, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them) that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Whoever receives sustenance from Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, without having walked towards it with his feet, nor extended his hand for it, nor spoken about it with his tongue, nor prepared himself for it by tightening his garments, nor exposed himself to acquire it-he is among those whom Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, mentioned in His Book:
'And whoever fears Allah (swt), He will make a way out for him, and will provide for him from where he does not expect.'" (Surah At-Talaq 65:2-3)

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3613 - Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "Assistance descends from the heavens in proportion to one's needs."
records.arabicText: 3613 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْمَعُونَةُ تَنْزِلُ مِنَ اَلسَّمَاءِ عَلَى قَدْرِ اَلْمَئُونَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3613 - Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Assistance descends from the heavens in proportion to one's needs."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3614 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Wealth that prevents you from committing oppression is better than poverty that leads you to sin."
records.arabicText: 3614 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "غِنًى يَحْجُزُكَ عَنِ اَلظُّلْمِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ فَقْرٍ يَحْمِلُكَ عَلَى اَلْإِثْمِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3614 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Wealth that prevents you from committing oppression is better than poverty that leads you to sin."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3615 - Imam (as) said: "There is no good in one who does not desire to earn wealth through lawful means, so that he may protect his dignity, pay off his debts, and maintain ties of kinship."
records.arabicText: 3615 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ خَيْرَ فِيمَنْ لاَ يُحِبُّ جَمْعَ اَلْمَالِ مِنْ حَلاَلٍ فَيَكُفَّ بِهِ وَجْهَهُ وَ يَقْضِيَ بِهِ دَيْنَهُ وَ يَصِلَ بِهِ رَحِمَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3615 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no good in one who does not desire to earn wealth through lawful means, so that he may protect his dignity, pay off his debts, and maintain ties of kinship."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3616 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Part of honor and dignity is managing wealth properly."
records.arabicText: 3616 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مِنَ اَلْمُرُوءَةِ اِسْتِصْلاَحُ اَلْمَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3616 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Part of honor and dignity is managing wealth properly."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3617 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Managing wealth properly is a part of faith."
records.arabicText: 3617 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِصْلاَحُ اَلْمَالِ مِنَ اَلْإِيمَانِ".
